title: Alternatives in regular expressions should be grouped when used with anchors
description: When using alternatives (|) in regular expressions with anchors (^ or $), the alternatives should be grouped with parentheses to ensure the anchors apply to the entire group, not just individual alternatives. Without proper grouping, the regex may not behave as intended.
recommendation: Group the alternatives with parentheses when using anchors to ensure the anchors apply to the entire group: /^(option1|option2)$/ instead of /^option1|option2$/
impact: Incorrect regex behavior and maintainability issues

checks.message: Group alternatives with parentheses when using anchors. Use /^(option1|option2)$/ instead of /^option1|option2$/ to ensure anchors apply to the entire group.
